raw_text:
Senior DevOps Engineer

CloudVortex Systems — Accelerating infrastructure automation for the next generation of cloud-native

enterprises

Location

Austin, TX (Hybrid)

Employment Type

Full-Time

Compensation

$165,000 - $210,000 annually

The Opportunity

We're seeking an experienced Senior DevOps Engineer to join our Infrastructure Platform team. You will

architect and maintain cloud infrastructure at scale, mentor junior engineers, lead infrastructure

modernization initiatives, and drive best practices across our engineering organization. This role requires

deep expertise in containerization, orchestration, infrastructure-as-code, and cloud platforms, with the
ability to make strategic technical decisions that impact our entire platform.

About CloudVortex Systems

CloudVortex Systems is a Series B infrastructure automation company founded in 2018, headquartered
in Austin, Texas. We specialize in helping mid-market to enterprise organizations modernize their cloud
infrastructure through intelligent automation, continuous deployment pipelines, and infrastructure-as-

code solutions. Our platform serves over 300 customers across North America, with a growing team of
120+ engineers committed to simplifying complex DevOps workflows.

Day-to-Day

Design, implement, and maintain highly available Kubernetes clusters and orchestration strategies

across AWS and GCP, supporting production workloads for 300+ customers

Lead infrastructure modernization projects using Terraform and GitOps principles, establishing IaC
standards and best practices across engineering teams

Architect and optimize CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions and Jenkins, reducing deployment times
and improving deployment reliability across 50+ microservices

Develop advanced Bash and Python automation scripts to streamline infrastructure provisioning,
monitoring, and disaster recovery procedures

Mentor 2-3 junior and mid-level DevOps engineers, conducting code reviews, technical interviews,
and knowledge-sharing sessions on infrastructure best practices

Collaborate with security and platform teams to implement zero-trust networking, RBAC policies, and

compliance automation across AWS/GCP environments

Lead on-call rotation as primary incident commander, managing production incidents and

implementing long-term solutions to prevent recurrence

 Conduct capacity planning and cost optimization reviews, identifying opportunities to reduce cloud

spend while maintaining performance SLAs

Own the design and documentation of disaster recovery and backup strategies, ensuring RTO/RPO

targets align with customer SLAs

You Have

6-8 years of professional DevOps, SRE, or infrastructure engineering experience, with at least 3 years

in a senior or lead technical role

Deep hands-on expertise with Kubernetes production deployment, cluster administration, and

troubleshooting at scale

Expert-level proficiency with Infrastructure-as-Code tools, specifically Terraform, including module

design and state management

Strong experience with AWS and/or GCP, including VPC design, IAM policies, RDS/Cloud SQL, and

container registries

Advanced proficiency in Bash scripting and Python for infrastructure automation and tooling

Demonstrated experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, Jenkins,
or similar platforms

Strong understanding of networking, DNS, SSL/TLS, and Linux system administration

Proven ability to mentor junior engineers and contribute to technical documentation and knowledge
sharing

Bonus

Experience with Helm, Kustomize, or other Kubernetes package managers and templating tools

Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, or

Datadog

Background in containerization best practices and Docker image optimization

Experience with GitOps frameworks such as ArgoCD or Flux for declarative deployments

Certifications such as CKA (Certified Kubernetes Administrator) or AWS Solutions Architect

Professional

Our Stack

Kubernetes   Docker   Terraform   AWS   GCP   GitHub Actions   Jenkins   Bash/Shell Scripting   Python   Helm

What We Offer

Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance with 90% premium coverage for employees

401(k) matching up to 5% with immediate vesting

Unlimited paid time off with a minimum of 20 days encouraged

Professional development budget of $2,500 annually for conferences, certifications, and training

 Home office stipend of $1,500 for remote work setup and quarterly equipment refreshes

Flexible work schedule and hybrid arrangement (3 days in-office Austin HQ, 2 days remote)

Comprehensive mental health support including therapy stipend and wellness programs

Stock options for early employees with 4-year vesting schedules

Apply

Submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your most complex infrastructure project, and a portfolio

or GitHub link showcasing your Terraform/Kubernetes work to careers@cloudvortex.io. Alternatively,

apply through our careers page at www.cloudvortex.io/careers. We review applications on a rolling basis

and typically respond within 5 business days. Candidates will participate in a technical screening call,

architecture design exercise, and culture fit interview.
nice_to_have: Experience with Helm, Kustomize, or other Kubernetes package managers and templating tools
Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, or
